title: Seed Registry, Origin Scope, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Type-System Trace: Origin Policy
summary: A seed silently expands through subdomains, downloads, calendars, search forms, query permutations, and logout URLs without page, byte, time, or incident limits. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts origin policy increment in deny-by-default crawl-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Define allowed schemes, hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default admission.
Bind each seed to owner authorization, purpose, expected content class, policy version, contact, and expiry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Origin Policy · Theory Origin Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 8623 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit authorized seeds through origin, path, query, multiresource budget, stop, and contact policy; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Competency probe: Define allowed schemes, hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default admission.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Origin Policy model · Theory Origin Policy, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Sharing a registrable domain always means sharing authority and risk.
